grammatical: Oscar heeft de atleet de marathon zien lopen afgelopen weekend.
ungrammatical: Oscar heeft de atleet de marathon lopen zien afgelopen weekend.
source: Suijkerbuijk et al. (2024), BLiMP-NL Phenomenon 21, item 801. https://europepmc.org/article/ppr/ppr837942
notes: Canonical BLiMP-NL item; human-crafted original. V1 appears in infinitive form due to IPP in AcI construction (heeft ... zien lopen).

grammatical: Oscar heeft de atleet de marathon zien lopen afgelopen weekend.
ungrammatical: Oscar heeft de marathon de atleet zien lopen afgelopen weekend.
source: Suijkerbuijk et al. (2024), BLiMP-NL Phenomenon 21, item 801. https://europepmc.org/article/ppr/ppr837942
notes: Ungrammaticality driven by selectional restriction: de marathon (inanimate) cannot be accusative object-subject of AcI perception verb. In Type 3 the argument structure of heeft + perception verb is also violated (inanimate NP in accusative-experiencer position), making the ungram_source slightly more complex than in Types 1 and 2. Do not use for alignment-only analysis without caveat.

grammatical: Oscar heeft de atleet de marathon zien lopen afgelopen weekend.
ungrammatical: Oscar heeft de marathon de atleet lopen zien afgelopen weekend.
source: Suijkerbuijk et al. (2024), BLiMP-NL Phenomenon 21, item 801. https://europepmc.org/article/ppr/ppr837942
notes: Alignment restored by double reversal but selectional restriction still violated (de marathon in accusative-experiencer position). Variant C interpretive caveat applies for Type 3: cannot cleanly dissociate alignment from argument structure. Use for patching experiments with caveat; cleanest Variant C signal comes from Types 1 and 2.
